task_completion.description: Open the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image in ImageJ/Fiji.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Blobs' image window (typically named 'blobs.gif') is visible in the ImageJ/Fiji workspace.

task_completion.description: Measure the mean intensity in the four quadrants of the original image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rectangular ROI (Region of Interest) selections are visible in the top-left, top-right, bottom-left, and bottom-right of the original image, and a 'Results' table shows the corresponding mean intensity measurements.

task_completion.description: Perform pseudo-flat-field correction to fix uneven illumination.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence of flat-field correction steps, such as a heavily blurred duplicate of the image (background), the 'Image Calculator' dialog (e.g., dividing or subtracting the original by the background), or a resulting image window showing the blobs with a noticeably more uniform background illumination.

task_completion.description: Measure the mean intensity in the four quadrants of the corrected image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rectangular ROI selections are visible in the quadrants of the newly corrected image, with new measurement rows added to the 'Results' table.

task_completion.description: Save the corrected image as 'flatfield_corrected.tif' in the results directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or file manager window showing the file 'flatfield_corrected.tif' being saved to or existing in the '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Save the CSV report with the required columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet program, text editor, or ImageJ save dialog showing a file named 'illumination_report.csv' being saved to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/'. The file contents or table must show columns 'Quadrant', 'Mean_Before', and 'Mean_After'.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ/Fiji GUI to perform the image processing tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with ImageJ/Fiji menus, toolbars, and dialogs (e.g., Image Calculator, Gaussian Blur) rather than using terminal commands or Python scripts to process the image.

integrity.description: The agent actually measured the quadrants rather than fabricating the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ImageJ 'Results' window contains actual measurement data corresponding to the ROI selections, which are then transferred to the CSV report, rather than the agent just typing arbitrary numbers directly into a text editor.

integrity.description: The corrected image is genuinely a flat-field corrected version of the original.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final 'flatfield_corrected.tif' image should visually display the same blob structures as the original but with a flattened/uniform background, not just a duplicate of the original image or a blank canvas.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is 'Blobs (25K)', a built-in ImageJ/Fiji sample image with dimensions 256x254 pixels. The task requires measuring 4 specific quadrants (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right). The output image must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/flatfield_corrected.tif'. The output report must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/illumination_report.csv'.
